items.title: Enhance Scene Transitions with Crossfades in Platform Studio 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces crossfade transitions for video and audio, allowing for smoother scene changes in rendered outputs. The `run_fastlane` function now supports a `transition` parameter, enabling users to specify the duration of crossfades, ensuring that audio remains in sync with the video. Additionally, tests have been added to verify the functionality and maintain code quality.

items.title: Digital Human Service Live in Index 🚀
items.summary: The digital human service has been successfully deployed to the production database, making it live on the platform. Key features include Service/Api/Document/Translation/Package with billing capabilities and whitelisted tasks in the gateway. The only remaining task is to establish a stable public tunnel and finalize the Kong binding.

items.title: Enhance API Access for Digital Human Tasks in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the `/digital-human/tasks` API UUID to the `SKIP_AUTH_API_IDS`, allowing the free poll endpoint to bypass authentication and billing. This change aligns it with other `/tasks` endpoints, improving accessibility for users. The modification ensures a smoother experience when accessing task data.

items.title: Exciting New Digital Human Service Registered in Backend 🤖
items.summary: We've successfully registered the new digital human service, enabling offline talking-head video generation. This update includes cost files for various tasks and comprehensive OpenAPI specifications for three key endpoints. The service is now synced with the database, enhancing our platform's capabilities.

items.title: Deployment Status & Handoff for Digital Human in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update documents the successful productionization run for the Digital Human project, including the local worker build and end-to-end verification with real videos. Key components are now blocked on operator infrastructure and credentials, with detailed next steps provided. The deployment status has been thoroughly recorded to ensure a smooth handoff.

items.title: Deploy Runbook and OpenAPI Specs for Digital Human in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a deploy runbook for the Digital Human service, including a one-command script to start the tunnel. Additionally, it adds OpenAPI specifications for videos, voices, and tasks, enhancing the create-service flow. These changes streamline the deployment process and improve API documentation for better integration.

items.title: Enhance Video Experience with Ken Burns and Captions in Platform Studio 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces Ken Burns effects for still images, allowing for a slow zoom that enhances visual storytelling. Additionally, burned-in captions are now rendered for each scene, catering to the 85% of social media viewers who watch videos on mute. A working music bed has also been implemented, ensuring that audio tracks are correctly fetched and integrated into the video output.

items.title: Introducing Offline Talking-Head API in Platform Service 🤖
items.summary: We've launched a new digitalhuman service that enables an offline talking-head API worker. This service orchestrates the local HeyGem (voice cloning and TTS) and LatentSync (lip-sync) containers on a single GPU, providing endpoints for voice cloning, video creation, and task polling. The implementation is designed for efficiency, allowing one GPU job at a time and ensuring high-quality output.

items.title: Fix media pricing regression in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a pricing regression in the public models and pricing directory caused by the recent grok-video and grok merge. The fix ensures that the video model's credit range is correctly scoped to its own rules, preventing chat pricing from affecting video pricing. New tests have been added to verify the changes, and all existing tests have passed successfully.

items.title: Enhance Asset Engine with Async Video Support in Platform Studio 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a robust asset engine in AceClient, enabling per-provider parameters and asynchronous video clip polling. Key changes include the implementation of `generate_media()` for handling both synchronous image and asynchronous video submissions, along with comprehensive unit tests for new helper functions. The functionality has been live-verified with seedance clips and seedream stills, ensuring a seamless integration into the production environment.

items.title: Enhance User Lookup with Account Type and Verification Date in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The admin user-query panel now displays the account type (personal or enterprise) and the verification timestamp, enhancing the user lookup experience. This update integrates data from the AuthBackend without requiring backend changes, ensuring seamless functionality. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for 12 locales, ensuring broad accessibility.

items.title: Clean up legacy verification columns in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the obsolete is_company_verified and company_verified_at columns from the app_user model, following the completion of the verification unification phase. Migration 0039 executes a DB-only command to drop these columns, ensuring a cleaner database structure. The changes have been verified with successful checks on migrations and code formatting.

items.title: Align Digital-Human API with Platform Contracts in Index 🤖
items.summary: This update rewrites the digital-human API section to align with existing platform service contracts, ensuring consistency across endpoints. Key changes include the restructuring of `POST /digital-human/videos`, `POST /digital-human/voices`, and `POST /digital-human/tasks` to match the input/output formats of related services. The new design incorporates standard response envelopes and error handling, enhancing the overall robustness of the API.

items.title: Enhance Service Access Management in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive UI for managing service access requests, allowing customers to apply for access and admins to review and approve requests. Key features include a new access widget on service detail pages, an admin console for managing access policies and requests, and support for 12 locales in the i18n configuration. The changes ensure a seamless experience for both users and administrators in handling service access.

items.title: Implement Layer-2 Whitelist Enforcement in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a runtime gate for service access in the authentication process, complementing the existing provisioning controls. Key changes include the addition of `AccessPolicy` and `AccessGrant` models, along with caching mechanisms to enhance performance for both gated and non-gated services. The implementation ensures that only users with the appropriate grants can access restricted services, improving overall security without impacting the user experience.

items.title: Enhance Access Control with Whitelist Feature in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a general service whitelist feature, allowing services to restrict access based on defined policies. Key components include new models for AccessPolicy, AccessGrant, and AccessRequest, which facilitate user membership management and service gating. The implementation ensures that only users with active grants can access restricted services, enhancing overall security and control.

items.title: Exciting New CLI Packages for Dreamina and Grok in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two new CLI packages: Dreamina and Grok. The Dreamina CLI allows users to generate AI talking-head videos and manage tasks, while the Grok CLI facilitates chat and video generation functionalities. Both packages adhere to existing monorepo conventions and include comprehensive documentation and tests.

items.title: Update service ID to merged grok service in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update repoints the `GROKVIDEO_SERVICE_ID` from the deprecated `grok-video` service to the newly merged `grok` service. The API paths remain unchanged, ensuring that the video page in Studio continues to function correctly without disruption. This change is crucial for maintaining service integrity following the backend migration.

items.title: Enhance CI to Prevent PR Cannibalization in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a concurrency group in the CI workflow to prevent overlapping runs from interfering with each other. By setting `cancel-in-progress: false`, new dispatches will queue behind ongoing runs, ensuring that each run can complete its tasks without being prematurely terminated. This change addresses issues observed in previous runs, leading to more reliable PR merges.

items.title: Polished Profile Page Layout and Badges in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update enhances the profile page by fixing label wrapping issues, optimizing layout, and reducing banner size. Labels now fit on a single line, and the connected badges for third-party accounts are visually emphasized with a green checkmark. These changes improve the overall user experience and maintain consistent spacing throughout the interface.

items.title: Enhance WebExtrator's Stability and Accuracy in Web Extrator 🚀
items.summary: This update significantly improves the stability and accuracy of the WebExtrator service. Key changes include a shift from `networkidle` to `load` for rendering, enhanced error handling, and improved extraction methods for markdown and Amazon products. Additionally, new observability features and tests have been added to ensure ongoing reliability.

items.title: Enhance Dreamina Tasks API Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update completes the Dreamina Tasks API documentation, adding a new OpenAPI spec and comprehensive guides in both English and Chinese. Key changes include a detailed introduction, enriched API usage instructions, and improved service visuals. The enhancements ensure users have a clear understanding of the API's capabilities and how to integrate it effectively.

items.title: Streamline Grok Service by Merging Video Features in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update merges the standalone `grok-video` service into the existing `grok` service, simplifying the user experience by consolidating chat and video functionalities. Key changes include reassigning API paths, updating documentation, and removing redundant service entries while ensuring existing API callers remain unaffected. The integration enhances clarity and maintains consistent pricing structures across services.

items.title: Production Plan for Talking-Head API Service in Index 🤖
items.summary: This update outlines the production design for the talking-head API service, transforming the validated PoC into a sellable async API on platform.acedata.cloud. Key features include a GPU worker pipeline, async API endpoints, and built-in quality guardrails. The design document has been added, detailing the architecture, billing, and licensing considerations.

items.title: Revitalize CLI Sync Pipelines in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes two critical issues in the CLI sync processes. The first issue involved a failure to detect completion signals from Copilot, leading to unmerged PRs. The second issue was due to an expired token that caused all mirrors to freeze. Both problems have been addressed to ensure smooth operation and timely updates across the CLI repositories.

items.title: Streamline Verification with Tabs in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update merges the personal and enterprise verification processes into a single tabbed interface within `Verify.vue`. Users can now easily switch between personal and enterprise verification, with the backend model updated to support this unified approach. The changes enhance user experience by simplifying navigation and reducing redundancy in the verification flow.

items.title: Enhance Social Signup Experience in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update promotes one-click social signup options, placing GitHub, Google, and Apple at the forefront of the registration process. Additionally, 'Sign in with Apple' has been added to the registration page, ensuring parity with the login experience. These changes aim to reduce onboarding friction and improve user experience for iOS users.

items.title: Introducing glm-5.2 to the chat-completion stack in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the glm-5.2 model to the chat-completion stack, enhancing the capabilities of our service. Key changes include updated billing rules and pricing mirroring glm-5.1, ensuring consistency. The model has been verified live on multiple platforms, confirming its functionality and readiness for use.

items.title: Exciting Update: Added glm-5.2 to Upstream Routing in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces glm-5.2 to the GLM upstream routing for the OpenAI service, enhancing model availability across several configurations. The new model is enabled and actively checked for ephone and xiaojingai, while openaihk and wcnb will mirror glm-5.1 until upstream issues are resolved. The checker cronjob will automatically sync these capabilities into MongoDB after the merge.

items.title: Enhance User Account Type Propagation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `account_type` attribute to the `User` model, allowing differentiation between personal and enterprise accounts. The `account_type` is now propagated from the AuthBackend, ensuring that future code can utilize this distinction. Key changes include modifications to the JWT and Token authentication processes to accommodate the new attribute without requiring database migrations.

items.title: Enhance Mobile Profile Usability in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses critical usability issues on the mobile profile page. Key changes include making edit icons always visible for touch devices, implementing a mobile drawer navigation to improve layout, and centering the avatar to prevent clipping. These enhancements ensure a smoother user experience for mobile users accessing their profiles.

items.title: Streamline Verification Process with Unified Flags in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the verification flags into a single `is_verified` attribute, along with an `account_type` field to distinguish between personal and enterprise accounts. The changes simplify the verification logic and ensure consistent behavior across the application. Additionally, a new migration has been added to handle data transitions smoothly while maintaining backward compatibility.

items.title: Pin Scene-JSON Schema for Reliable Outputs in Platform Studio 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where the real Claude script stage failed due to mismatched field names in the Scene-JSON schema. The schema is now explicitly defined in the SYSTEM_PROMPT, ensuring that the correct field names are used, which allows the fast lane to run successfully. Additionally, a regression test has been added to verify the schema's integrity against future changes.

items.title: Enhance Coding Bridge UI with Syntax Highlighting and Mobile Improvements in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces several key UI/UX enhancements for the Coding Bridge feature in Nexior. Syntax highlighting has been added to tool command blocks, and a new copy button functionality has been implemented for easier code sharing. Additionally, the mobile interface has been improved by relocating the device entry point to the header, and a loading skeleton has been restored for better user experience during data fetching.

items.title: Improve StoreKit Product Polling in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the product loading process by implementing a polling mechanism for StoreKit products before failing. Instead of failing immediately with a 'product_not_found' error, the code now waits up to 15 seconds to check for the offer, addressing issues related to asynchronous loading and sandbox propagation. This change aims to improve user experience during product purchases.

items.title: Fix mobile app-list Top Up and global card overflow in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves issues with the mobile layout of the applications list, specifically ensuring the Buy More button functions correctly by referencing the appropriate method. Additionally, it addresses text overflow in the global-balance card by applying a minimum width to allow proper wrapping. These changes enhance the user experience on mobile devices.

items.title: Enhance Count Unit Translations in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update adds missing translations for the `Count` service unit across 18 locales, ensuring that users see the correct terms instead of raw keys. Key additions include translations for both singular and plural forms, enhancing the user experience for services like the WeChat Bot. All changes have passed coverage checks.

items.title: Updated Company Address in Platform Service 📍
items.summary: The company address has been updated across the static introduction page footers for the captcha, luma, and suno applications. The new address is now Suite 305, 131 Continental Dr, Newark, DE 19713, USA, replacing the previous location in Middletown, Delaware. This change ensures that all references to the company address are current and accurate.

items.title: Updated Company Address in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The company address has been updated in the privacy policy and support pages for the zh-CN locale. The new address is now 131 Continental Dr, Suite 305, Newark, DE 19713, USA, replacing the previous address in Middletown, Delaware. This change will automatically reflect across other locales through the existing documentation pipeline.

items.title: Updated Company Address to Newark, DE in Roadmap 📍
items.summary: The company address has been updated in the README file. The old address in Middletown, Delaware has been replaced with the new address located at 131 Continental Dr, Suite 305, Newark, DE 19713, USA. This change ensures that all communications reflect the current location.

items.title: Updated Company Address to Newark, DE in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the company address in the footer across all locale files, changing it from Middletown to Newark, DE. The new address is now reflected in 18 different language files, ensuring that the street, suite, city, and ZIP code are accurately localized. All JSON files have been verified for validity.

items.title: Updated Company Address in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the company address in the footer across all locale files for the PlatformFrontend. The address has been changed from '651/201 North Broad St, Suite 201, Middletown, DE, USA' to '131 Continental Dr, Suite 305, Newark, DE 19713, USA'. All 12 language files have been updated to reflect this change while maintaining localized country and state names.

items.title: Update Fish TTS Naming for Clarity in Backend 🎤
items.summary: This update corrects the naming of the Fish voice and TTS service, ensuring it is accurately represented in the documentation. Key changes include fixing references from 'music' to 'voice' in the zh-CN integration docs and updating display titles in the Translation DB for immediate correctness. These adjustments enhance clarity for users and maintain consistency across languages.

items.title: Upgrade axios to version 1.18.0 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps axios from version 1.17.0 to 1.18.0, enhancing security by improving redirect header safety and URL handling. Additionally, it introduces new validation options and updates various dependencies to ensure better performance and maintainability.
